updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.212: /fork Redesign, Auto-Mode Limits & MCP Auto-Background
updates.summary: Claude Code v2.1.212 shipped at 00:26 UTC on July 17, delivering a major redesign of the /fork command — it now copies conversations into new background sessions (visible in claude agents) while keeping current work active, with the old in-session subagent behavior moved to /subtask. The release also introduces a new claude auto-mode reset command, session-wide WebSearch and subagent spawn caps (default 200 each, configurable), automatic backgrounding of MCP tool calls that exceed 2 minutes, and a /resume picker for reopening past and deleted sessions. Thirty-plus bug fixes address plan-mode auto-run permissions, SIGTERM process orphaning, Windows PowerShell Group Policy conflicts, and OpenTelemetry trace-ID gaps.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from official Claude Code GitHub Releases page and docs.claude.com changelog. Version number, release time (00:26 UTC July 17), and full feature list cross-verified. All changes are code-level and verifiable. No speculative claims.

Slide 4: MCP tools that run >2 min auto-move to background (no more freezes)

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.211: Subagent Text Streaming, Security Hardening & 25+ Fixes
updates.summary: Claude Code v2.1.211, released at 23:02 UTC on July 15, adds the --forward-subagent-text flag and CLAUDE_CODE_FORWARD_SUBAGENT_TEXT environment variable so subagent text and thinking appear in stream-json output — a key developer-facing addition for build pipelines. Two security fixes address permission-preview character spoofing (bidirectional-override, zero-width, look-alike quotes) and a bug where auto mode overrode PreToolUse hook 'ask' decisions for unsandboxed Bash. Over 25 additional fixes improve parallel session credential stability, plugin MCP reconnection after idle, file upload validation (DOS device suffixes), Chrome upload handling, and background session reliability.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from official Claude Code GitHub Releases page and docs.claude.com changelog. Version number, release time (23:02 UTC July 15), feature list, and security fixes cross-verified. Security issues described are real CVE-class behaviors (character injection in permission previews). No speculative claims.

updates.title: Anthropic Adds Self-Serve HIPAA Configuration for Enterprise & API Orgs
updates.summary: Anthropic rolled out self-serve HIPAA configuration for Claude Enterprise and Claude Platform (API) organizations on July 15, replacing the previous sales-assisted process. Eligible admins can now review the Business Associate Agreement (BAA), download the implementation guide, and enable HIPAA compliance in a single flow inside their org settings. This makes Claude accessible to healthcare and life-sciences teams without requiring a sales engagement, significantly lowering the barrier for regulated-industry adoption.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via search results referencing Releasebot Anthropic updates for July 15 and the Claude Help Center HIPAA article. The feature description (self-serve BAA + guide download + one-step enablement) is consistent across multiple sources. Applicability limited to US HIPAA context — international compliance frameworks not mentioned.

updates.title: Anthropic, Blackstone & Hellman-Friedman Launch 'Ode with Anthropic' — $1.5B Enterprise AI Services Firm
updates.summary: On July 15, Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs announced 'Ode with Anthropic,' a $1.5 billion standalone enterprise AI services company built to embed Claude-powered AI engineers directly inside mid-market and enterprise businesses. Ode operates on a 'Claude-first' principle and was founded on the operational foundation of Fractional AI (acquired by Anthropic in May 2026). The move mirrors OpenAI's parallel 'Deployment Company' initiative and signals a growing belief among frontier AI labs that winning enterprise customers requires forward-deployed implementation teams — not just better models.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via multiple independent sources: BusinessWire press release (July 15, 2026), TechCrunch, HPCwire, Yahoo Finance, and Anthropic's official newsroom. Business details (Blackstone, H&F, Goldman Sachs, $1.5B, Claude-first, Fractional AI foundation) consistent across all sources. Note: this update originates from anthropic.com/news rather than the docs.anthropic.com release notes source — included due to its direct relevance (Anthropic company news) and 24-48h publication window.
